subject: English
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: NOTE SUMMARY
topics:  Overview: A note summary is a set of points or notes obtained from a passage or an
oral source. It is a collection of all the important points. These points are listed. The
points do not need to be written in full sentences. Abbreviations may also be used.
activities:  Pair learners and ask them to name sources of information.
Oral sources: Television, YOUTUBE videos, Radio, Speeches
Written sources: Newspapers, Articles, Journals, Magazines, Textbooks
 Explain that we take notes from an oral source, while we make notes from a written
source i.e. Note making and Note taking.
Note making is the process of writing down important information in a short and
organised form for future reference from a written source.
Note taking is the process of quickly taking down information as you listen to an oral
resources: major drivers of biodiversity loss. The consequences of biodiversity are far
reaching and can have significant impact on human well-being.
Use the following questions to make note summaries of the passage.
What are the benefits of biodiversity?
assessment:  Observe learners as lesson is progressing to monitor their progress and understanding of
 Learners to write note summaries by giving them individual exercise.
 There are two types of note summaries which are note- making and note taking.
 Note making entails making note from a written source whereas note-taking entails
taking note from an oral source.
